findingDetails.title: md-generator output guard does not enforce the documented spec-folder or sandbox boundary
findingDetails.claim: The md-generator backend can write generated artifacts outside the documented spec-folder or sandbox boundary because it only rejects paths inside the skill directory, then writes artifacts under any other caller-provided output path.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked URL-derived screenshot filenames and found urlToSlug strips separators/non-alphanumeric characters; no URL-controlled traversal was confirmed.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The backend may intentionally allow arbitrary operator-selected output directories, but then the documented spec-folder/sandbox boundary is inaccurate and needs explicit confirmation semantics.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the intended contract is changed to explicitly allow arbitrary operator-selected output paths and command UX adds clear confirmation/rollback language before non-spec writes.

findingDetails.title: Extracted live-site values enter the WRITE prompt without prompt-data isolation
findingDetails.claim: A malicious or compromised source site can inject instruction-like text into the md-generator WRITE prompt through extracted typography values because the prompt builder interpolates live-site-derived strings directly into a natural-language prompt without delimiting or escaping them as untrusted data.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked fidelity cards and hard rules; they prevent hallucinated values but do not isolate live-site strings from prompt instructions or sanitize control/Markdown-like content before the WRITE prompt is consumed.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Most real CSS font-family values are benign and CSS parsing constrains some characters, reducing likelihood but not removing the live-site-to-AI-prompt trust-boundary risk.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if prompt construction serializes all extracted values in a fenced/escaped data-only block, tests cover malicious font/text values, and the writer prompt tells the model to treat extracted content as data only.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: The security pass covers a multi-mode skill hub, a mutating Playwright backend, command frontmatters, and spec/playbook output-boundary contracts.

selectedTargets: WRITE prompt builder and upstream token collectors
mode registry and design command frontmatters
targetSelection.selectionReason: Targets were selected from the iteration security focus and prior strategy risk list: path/output guards, Bash/process boundaries, prompt injection, generated artifact writes, and read-only vs mutating tool boundaries.
omittedHighRiskTargets: full report/preview/proof writer audit beyond shared output-dir guard

searchLedger.rationale: Docs/playbook describe spec-folder or sandbox output boundaries, but implementation accepts any non-skill output path and writes artifacts there.
searchLedger.hypothesis: Generated artifact writes are insufficiently scoped to documented output boundaries.

searchActions.result: Located all backend write sites and output guards.

searchActions.queryOrPath: extraction workflow and manual playbook output policy
searchActions.result: Confirmed spec-folder and sandbox policy language.

searchLedger.rationale: The guided wrapper uses spawnSync with argv arrays and no shell option; user URL/output values are passed as arguments, not shell-interpolated command strings.
searchLedger.invariant: No shell interpolation should sit between user URL/output input and npx/ts-node execution.

searchActions.result: Found spawnSync usage only in guided-run.ts; no exec/shell true path confirmed.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No command-injection path was confirmed because execution uses argv arrays with default shell=false.

searchLedger.rationale: Live-site-derived typography values are interpolated into the AI WRITE prompt without data-only serialization or prompt escaping.
searchLedger.hypothesis: Extracted site content can become prompt instructions during the WRITE phase.

searchActions.queryOrPath: dom collector -> cluster -> build-write-prompt data path
searchActions.result: Confirmed font/text capture, quote-only stripping, and direct FACTS interpolation.

searchLedger.rationale: The crawler normalizes URL inputs, keeps discovered links on the same hostname, and skips forms/submits/navigation/action-intent controls before clicking reveal UI.
searchLedger.invariant: Crawler interactions should not submit forms, navigate through action links, or traverse off-domain during discovery.

searchActions.queryOrPath: crawl.ts link discovery and modal trigger checks
searchActions.result: Same-domain filtering and state-changing action skips were present in the sampled code.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No crawler side-effect vulnerability was confirmed in the sampled URL/link/click path.

searchLedger.rationale: Registry entries and command/mode frontmatters preserve Read/Glob/Grep-only boundaries for interface/foundations/motion/audit and reserve Write/Edit/Bash for md-generator.
searchLedger.invariant: Read-only design modes must not grant or imply Write/Edit/Bash authority.

searchActions.result: Found expected read-only command frontmatters and md-generator mutating frontmatter only.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No read-only mode escalation was confirmed in registry, mode frontmatter, or command frontmatter.

searchLedger.rationale: P1-001 does not currently add a component-facts prompt-injection path because component facts are missing rather than interpolated; however, its future remediation must apply data isolation.
searchLedger.invariant: The prior correctness gap should not be re-reported unless it creates a distinct current security issue.

searchActions.queryOrPath: iteration-002 P1-001 and build-write-prompt component instructions
searchActions.result: Confirmed component data is required by instructions but not emitted as facts today.

searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No separate current security finding for P1-001 was confirmed beyond the new broader prompt-data isolation finding.

title: md-generator output guard does not enforce the documented spec-folder or sandbox boundary
Extracted live-site values enter the WRITE prompt without prompt-data isolation

reason: spawnSync uses argv arrays with no shell option; no exec/shell interpolation was confirmed.
Same-domain link filtering and form/submit/navigation/action-intent skips were present in the sampled crawler path.
Registry and command frontmatters preserve read-only vs mutating tool surfaces.
The prior component-facts gap does not currently interpolate component facts into the WRITE prompt, so it is not a separate current security issue.

effect: Modified outside allowed write list after state append / verification path; observed diff updated dashboard counters from iteration 2/P1=1 to iteration 3/P1=3.
actionTaken: Recorded in iteration narrative and delta; did not edit or revert the dashboard because it is outside allowed write paths.

effect: Created outside allowed write list after state append / reducer path.
